name: democracy-in-america
description: Alexis de Tocqueville's classic — understanding the dynamics of democratic societies, the tyranny of the majority, the tension between liberty and equality, and the institutions that protect freedom.
useCase: Diagnosing groupthink, building civic associations, balancing liberty and equality, recognizing soft despotism, understanding democratic risk.
knownRisks: Tocqueville wrote about 1830s America — some observations may not apply directly to contemporary contexts
The book is deeply rooted in 19th century European political philosophy
Tocqueville's views on women, race, and colonialism reflect his time and can be problematic
